file_insights.description: Refactored run_dispatcher to use notify file watcher with 100ms debounce instead of polling on pending/ directory. Enables event-driven card processing model (WatchPaths instead of polling loop).
file_insights.impact: Architectural shift from polling to event-driven dispatch mechanism

file_insights.description: Dispatch orchestration state machine tracking spec completion progression. Shows specs 018 (cli-ux-colors-stats) and 019 (serve-security-hardening) transitioned to completed queue.

Event-driven dispatcher specification (spec 020) - this subtask is directly implementing the core refactoring for this spec. Contains build progress, implementation plan, and session insights tracking.
file_insights.impact: Tracks progress of this subtask implementation

file_insights.description: QA approval recorded in implementation_plan.json with qa_signoff status 'approved' (459/459 unit tests passed). Indicates spec 018 reached QA completion.

patterns_discovered.evidence: Transition from polling-based dispatcher (implicit in previous code) to notify watcher with 100ms debounce (explicit in commit message). Aligns with MICROCLAW target architecture using WatchPaths events.
dispatch-state.json shows queue progression: specs 018-019 completed, implies 020 (event-driven-dispatcher) is current active spec. Session insights tracked per-spec.
Memory files created for sessions 015 and 016 (spec 018), sessions 001-009 (spec 019). Each contains attempt_history, build_commits, session_insights with timestamps.
Commit message explicitly specifies '100ms debounce' for pending/ watcher. Suggests careful timing selection to batch rapid file system events.

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Diff truncation obscures dispatcher.rs implementation details
gotchas_discovered.context: Git diff output marked '(truncated, 1088976 chars total)' - unable to inspect actual notify watcher setup, error handling, and integration with card state machine.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Review full crates/bop-cli/src/dispatcher.rs directly; verify debounce implementation and event filtering logic

gotchas_discovered.context: Multiple rapid card additions to pending/ will be collected into single dispatch cycle. Could mask ordering issues or race conditions.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Test with rapid card creation scenarios; monitor event ordering in logs; adjust debounce if needed

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: MICROCLAW target is not fully realized yet
gotchas_discovered.context: Memory notes indicate 'adapters/*.nu are stepping stones only — QEMU adapter is the end state' (Wave 5+). Dispatcher refactor is prerequisite but not final architecture.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Track Wave 5+ specifications; plan QEMU integration after dispatcher stabilizes

gotchas_discovered.context: notify crate behavior varies on macOS (FSEvents), Linux (inotify), Windows. Debounce tuning may need platform-specific overrides.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Test on target platform (macOS per MEMORY.md); monitor for event coalescing edge cases

approach_outcome.summary: Refactored run_dispatcher to use notify file watcher with 100ms debounce on pending/ directory. Core feature implemented as per spec 020 (event-driven-dispatcher). Related specs 018-019 advanced to QA completion status.
approach_outcome.critical_path: Dispatcher watcher implementation is prerequisite for Wave 5+ QEMU integration

recommendations.recommendation: Document debounce timing rationale in dispatcher.rs comments. Justify 100ms choice: fast enough for interactive use, slow enough to batch rapid creates. Include tuning guide for different workload profiles.
recommendations.rationale: Future maintainers need to understand timing trade-offs; may need adjustment for VM-based workflow with 9P mount events

recommendations.recommendation: Add integration test for rapid card creation scenario: create 10+ cards in <200ms window. Verify watcher batches into single dispatch cycle. Test on macOS (FSEvents coalescing).
recommendations.rationale: Event batching is critical for performance; race conditions possible under load

recommendations.recommendation: Instrument dispatcher with event count metrics: log number of pending/ events batched per dispatch cycle. Track debounce effectiveness (target: 2-5 events per cycle under normal load).
recommendations.rationale: Validates debounce tuning; early warning if file system events spike

recommendations.recommendation: Plan QEMU adapter integration (Wave 5+) as next dependency. Current dispatcher uses local notify watcher; QEMU version will need 9P event translation or polling fallback. Document protocol.
recommendations.rationale: Dispatcher refactor is stepping stone to MICROCLAW target; QEMU integration will require new event source handling

recommendations.recommendation: Add dispatcher debug mode: `--watch-verbose` flag to log all file system events, debounce intervals, and dispatch triggers. Helpful for diagnosing event batching issues.
recommendations.rationale: File system behavior is hard to debug; explicit logging of events aids root cause analysis

recommendations.recommendation: Update MEMORY.md architecture section with event-driven dispatcher entry point and notify watcher lifecycle. Clarify relationship to Wave 5 QEMU adapter.
recommendations.rationale: Architectural knowledge concentration; future wave planning depends on understanding current state
